features.evidence: I bought this MacBook to replace an aging Windows laptop I had, and I’ve been tickled to death with it—the screen is plenty large, it’s got plenty of RAM for everything I run concurrently (Internet, email, Microsoft Office, RustDesk), it only weighs a couple of pounds, and it seems like the battery just lasts for days (I typically use it for 30 minutes to 3 hours at a time).I am so thankful they brought back the MagSafe charger, I haven’t had anyone kick the cable and disconnect it from my laptop yet, If you want a lightweight laptop with a large display, powerful performance, and top-tier build quality, this MacBook Air is absolutely worth it.

features.evidence: So, even if you are in the Windows and Android camps like I am, definitely think about getting a macbook, cause there are still apps out there that let your android phone talk to MacOS like KDE Connect for example. I bought this MacBook to replace an aging Windows laptop I had, and I’ve been tickled to death with it—the screen is plenty large, it’s got plenty of RAM for everything I run concurrently (Internet, email, Microsoft Office, RustDesk), it only weighs a couple of pounds, and it seems like the battery just lasts for days (I typically use it for 30 minutes to 3 hours at a time).I am so thankful they brought back the MagSafe charger, I haven’t had anyone kick the cable and disconnect it from my laptop yet,

The build quality is solid, and the design looks modern and professional. If you want a lightweight laptop with a large display, powerful performance, and top-tier build quality, this MacBook Air is absolutely worth it.

The remedy is I just simply bought an inexpensive Bluetooth mouse and that works perfect, no extra dongles. The charging brick has two USB-C ports on it, so I can plug in a charging cable for my phone as well.

The performance is fast and smooth, and it handles daily tasks and multitasking without any issues. Performance is incredibly fast and smooth—everything from multitasking to creative work feels effortless.

I bought this MacBook to replace an aging Windows laptop I had, and I’ve been tickled to death with it—the screen is plenty large, it’s got plenty of RAM for everything I run concurrently (Internet, email, Microsoft Office, RustDesk), it only weighs a couple of pounds, and it seems like the battery just lasts for days (I typically use it for 30 minutes to 3 hours at a time).I am so thankful they brought back the MagSafe charger, I haven’t had anyone kick the cable and disconnect it from my laptop yet, With 16GB of unified memory, the system stays responsive even with multiple apps open, and the 256GB SSD delivers quick boot times and fast file access.

The screen as I mentioned in my other review on the 13inch version can be damaged by debris if you don't clean your keyboard and screen before closing because it's a tight space between the screen and the keyboard when close.
